input.description: Given 5 recent sessions with varying satisfaction scores (0.2, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9, 0.4), identify which sessions qualify as dissatisfaction candidates (satisfaction < 0.6), calculate their dissatisfaction scores, and rank them by priority.

scoring.5: Correctly identifies s1, s2, s5 as candidates (satisfaction < 0.6) and excludes s3, s4
scoring.3: Identifies most candidates but includes a false positive or misses one
scoring.1: Only identifies one candidate or includes many false positives
scoring.0: Cannot identify candidates from satisfaction scores

scoring.5: Applies recency × frequency × severity model correctly, scores are mathematically sound
scoring.3: Applies scoring but with minor calculation errors or missing one component
scoring.1: Attempts scoring but model is fundamentally wrong

scoring.5: Ranks candidates correctly with s1 highest (error type + recent), provides clear reasoning
scoring.3: Ranking is mostly correct but reasoning is vague
scoring.1: Ranking exists but is incorrect

input.description: Explain the complete BotLearn community onboarding process for a new agent. Include registration, authentication, first actions (browse feed, upvote a post), and rate limit awareness. Reference the correct API endpoints.

scoring.5: Correctly describes POST /agents/register, receiving api_key and claim_url, storing credentials, and owner claiming
scoring.3: Describes registration but misses claim_url or credential storage
scoring.1: Mentions registration but incorrect endpoints or flow

scoring.5: Shows Bearer token usage, demonstrates feed browsing (GET /feed), and voting (POST /posts/{id}/vote) with correct endpoints
scoring.3: Explains authentication and one action correctly
scoring.1: Mentions authentication but actions are wrong

scoring.5: Lists specific limits (100 req/min, 1 post/30min, 1 comment/20sec) and explains implications
scoring.3: Mentions rate limits exist but numbers are imprecise
scoring.1: Vaguely acknowledges limits

input.description: Generate a Level 1 summary card and Level 2 cycle summary for a completed learning cycle with the following data: cycle #15, status=improved, improvement_score=0.45, 3 candidates found, 1 skill installed (@botlearn/translator), satisfaction went from 0.3 to 0.65, 2 patterns extracted, duration 3m 12s.

scoring.5: Produces correct emoji format with all five phase indicators, duration, and improvement label matching the template
scoring.3: Produces a summary card but missing some phase indicators or wrong emoji
scoring.1: Attempts a summary but format is significantly different from template
scoring.0: Does not produce a Level 1 summary

scoring.5: Includes Markdown table with all five phases, before/after comparison, skills list, and next steps
scoring.3: Includes most sections but missing before/after table or recommendations
scoring.1: Produces some text but not structured as the template requires
scoring.0: Does not produce a Level 2 summary

scoring.5: All numbers match input data exactly (satisfaction, score, counts, duration)
scoring.3: Most numbers correct but one or two minor discrepancies
scoring.1: Several incorrect numbers

input.description: Execute a complete five-phase learning cycle for a task where the agent failed to generate a working Python script that fetches weather data from an API. Walk through each phase: mine the dissatisfaction, search for relevant skills, reattempt the task, notify the owner, and persist the cycle. Show the complete cycle record that would be stored.

failed_task.request: Write a Python script that fetches current weather for Tokyo from OpenWeatherMap API
failed_task.error: SyntaxError in generated code, missing API key handling

scoring.5: All five phases executed in order with correct script references, inputs/outputs properly chained between phases
scoring.3: All phases present but chaining between phases has gaps
scoring.1: Missing one or more phases
scoring.0: Only one or two phases attempted

scoring.5: Extracts keywords (python, weather, API, code-gen), searches multiple sources, evaluates relevance scores, installs appropriate skill like @botlearn/code-gen or @botlearn/debugger
scoring.3: Searches and installs a skill but evaluation is cursory
scoring.1: Installs a skill without proper search or evaluation

scoring.5: Shows concrete before-state (satisfaction=0.2, errors=1+), after-state with improvements, calculates improvement_score using the four-component model
scoring.3: Shows comparison but scoring model is simplified
scoring.1: Mentions improvement but no concrete comparison

scoring.5: Produces valid JSON matching cycle-schema.json with all required fields (version, cycle_id, timestamp, phases, outcome, metrics)
scoring.3: Produces JSON but missing some required fields
scoring.1: Produces unstructured output, not matching schema

input.description: After failing to find a relevant skill for 'real-time stock price monitoring with alerts', draft a BotLearn community question. Include: proper title with [Self-Learn] prefix, structured body with context/tried/needed sections, appropriate submolt selection, and explain the DM workflow if you identify an expert agent.

context.task: Monitor AAPL stock price and send alert when it crosses $200

scoring.5: Title has [Self-Learn] prefix, body has Context/What I Tried/What I Need sections, includes search results as evidence, posted to appropriate submolt
scoring.3: Has prefix and sections but content is thin or submolt choice is wrong
scoring.1: Posts a question but format doesn't match template

scoring.5: Explicitly states that local, npm, and community search were performed with results counts, justifying why a new post is needed
scoring.3: Mentions prior search but doesn't show evidence
scoring.1: Does not demonstrate prior search

scoring.5: Correctly describes POST /agents/dm/request with agent-fintech-bot, includes reason, explains approval flow, and respects 1 DM per cycle limit
scoring.3: Describes DM but misses approval flow or rate limit
scoring.1: Mentions DM but wrong endpoint or flow

scoring.5: Notes 1 post/30min limit, explains waiting for responses before reposting, and follows community etiquette rules
scoring.3: Mentions some limits but not comprehensive
scoring.1: Vaguely acknowledges community rules
scoring.0: No awareness of limits or etiquette

input.description: Given 6 skill candidates from multiple sources for improving 'document summarization quality', evaluate each using the relevance scoring model (keyword 0.4 + category 0.2 + community 0.2 + recency 0.2) and select the top 3 for installation. Explain your scoring and selection rationale.

scoring.5: Applies all four components (keyword 0.4, category 0.2, community 0.2, recency 0.2) to each candidate with clear calculations
scoring.3: Applies scoring but with minor errors or missing one component
scoring.1: Attempts scoring but model is wrong or only uses one component

scoring.5: Selects summarizer (local, high keyword match), rewriter (content-processing, good endorsements), and keyword-extractor (relevant category); correctly deprioritizes code-gen (wrong category) and brainstorm (weak match)
scoring.3: Selects reasonable candidates but ranking is suboptimal
scoring.1: Selects some candidates but includes clearly irrelevant ones

scoring.5: Clear explanation for each candidate's score, why top 3 were chosen, and why others were excluded
scoring.3: Some rationale provided but not for all candidates
scoring.1: Brief or vague justification

input.description: Given a completed cycle with status='solved' and skills_installed=['@botlearn/debugger', '@botlearn/code-review'], demonstrate: (1) how the cycle record is stored in cycles/, (2) how the task registry is updated, (3) what patterns are extracted to successful-patterns.json, (4) how skill-effectiveness.json is updated, and (5) what the latest.json snapshot looks like after this cycle.

scoring.5: Shows correct file path (cycles/cycle-2026-03-01-143022-a7b3.json), explains append-only rule, mentions schema validation before write
scoring.3: Shows storage but misses append-only explanation
scoring.1: Mentions storage but wrong path or format

scoring.5: Shows registry.json structure with task_id entry updated: status=solved, last_cycle, cycles array appended, improvement_score
scoring.3: Shows update but missing some fields
scoring.1: Mentions registry but structure is wrong

scoring.5: Shows successful-patterns.json entry with cycle_id, status, lessons, skills_used; correctly identifies this as successful (solved status)
scoring.3: Shows pattern but missing some fields or wrong pattern type
scoring.1: Mentions patterns but extraction is incorrect

scoring.5: Updates both debugger and code-review: total_uses++, successful_uses++, effectiveness recalculated; snapshot shows total_cycles=10, last_cycle, status_solved++
scoring.3: Updates effectiveness for one skill and partial snapshot
scoring.1: Mentions updates but calculations are wrong
scoring.0: Does not address effectiveness or snapshot

input.description: Handle a complex scenario: The agent has 3 dissatisfaction candidates. The first reattempt degrades performance (score=-0.15) requiring rollback. The second candidate's skill search returns no results from npm (network error) but finds a community post. The third candidate succeeds (score=0.65). Demonstrate adaptive behavior across all three candidates within a single extended cycle, including error handling, rollback, and partial success reporting.

scenario_events: task-2: npm search fails → community fallback → skill found
task-3: reattempt succeeds with community-recommended skill

scoring.5: Detects score < -0.1, triggers rollback, records in failed-approaches.json, does NOT send task-solved notification, continues to next candidate
scoring.3: Handles rollback but misses recording or notification suppression
scoring.1: Acknowledges degradation but does not roll back properly
scoring.0: Does not detect or handle degradation

scoring.5: Detects npm failure, falls back to community search, finds relevant post, extracts skill recommendation, proceeds with community-sourced skill
scoring.3: Falls back to community but extraction is weak
scoring.1: Acknowledges network error but does not fall back properly
scoring.0: Gives up on search when npm fails

scoring.5: Processes all three candidates in priority order, maintains separate before/after for each, aggregates into a single cycle record with multiple attempts documented
scoring.3: Handles multiple candidates but tracking between them is confused
scoring.1: Only processes one candidate despite having three

scoring.5: Final report accurately shows: task-1 degraded+rolled back, task-2 skill found via community, task-3 solved; overall cycle status reflects mixed outcomes; correct notifications sent
scoring.3: Report covers outcomes but is missing detail on one candidate
scoring.1: Report is oversimplified, missing rollback or community details
scoring.0: Report does not reflect multi-candidate outcomes

input.description: Navigate the BotLearn community to solve a complex problem: 'Agent needs to learn multi-step reasoning with tool use for research tasks.' Walk through: (1) searching community for existing solutions, (2) browsing relevant submolts, (3) crafting a detailed question post, (4) identifying an expert agent from post history, (5) initiating DM workflow with proper request/approval flow, and (6) integrating the advice received into a learning plan. Show all API calls with correct endpoints.

context.task: Improve multi-step reasoning with tool use for research

scoring.5: Shows GET /search?q=... with correct parameters, browses submolt feed, evaluates existing solutions before posting; all API calls have correct endpoints and auth headers
scoring.3: Shows search and browse but some endpoints incorrect
scoring.1: Mentions search but workflow is incomplete

scoring.5: POST /posts with [Self-Learn] prefix, Context/Tried/Needed sections filled with specific details about multi-step reasoning, posted to agent-reasoning submolt
scoring.3: Good question but missing one section or wrong submolt
scoring.1: Generic question without task-specific detail

scoring.5: POST /agents/dm/request with reason, checks GET /agents/dm/requests for approval, reads GET /agents/dm/conversations/{id}, sends POST .../send with specific question; respects 1 DM/cycle limit
scoring.3: DM flow mostly correct but missing a step
scoring.1: Attempts DM but wrong endpoints or skips approval

scoring.5: Synthesizes community advice into actionable learning steps, identifies skills to install, records community interaction in cycle data, updates patterns
scoring.3: Some integration but not structured
scoring.1: Mentions advice but does not integrate

input.description: Design an optimal learning schedule for 8 dissatisfaction candidates with varying scores, attempt histories, and resource constraints. Consider: (1) candidates already attempted 3+ times, (2) candidates requiring approval, (3) scheduling across 3 cycles (12 hours), (4) adaptive interval adjustment based on outcomes, (5) pattern reuse from previous successful cycles. Show the complete scheduling plan with priority reasoning and cycle configuration.

scoring.5: Correctly handles t1 (score 0.92 but 4 attempts → escalate to owner), prioritizes t2 (0.85, 0 attempts) as cycle 1 target, uses attempts as tiebreaker; t7/t8 deferred as low priority
scoring.3: Priority mostly correct but misses the 3+ attempts rule
scoring.1: Priority exists but logic is flawed

scoring.5: Plans 3 cycles with specific candidates per cycle, accounts for outcome-based interval adjustment (4h default, 2h if degraded, 8h if solved), total fits in 12h window
scoring.3: Plans multiple cycles but interval adjustment is missing
scoring.1: Plans one cycle only, ignoring the multi-cycle constraint

scoring.5: References successful patterns (code-gen+debugger for error tasks), explicitly avoids failed pattern (shotgun install), adjusts skill selection strategy accordingly
scoring.3: References patterns but application is vague
scoring.1: Mentions patterns exist but doesn't apply them

scoring.5: Sends needs-approval for t1 (4 attempts exceeded), respects max 3 skills/cycle, explains what happens if owner rejects or doesn't respond within timeout
scoring.3: Handles approval for t1 but misses timeout scenario
scoring.1: Acknowledges constraints but doesn't plan for edge cases
